title: One Shared #Hermes From Any Device (Local UI, VPS Brain)
summary: Keith AI demonstrates a distributed architecture where Hermes can be shared across multiple devices using a local UI connected to a remote VPS-hosted inference backend. This enables local interface access while centralizing model compute resources.
writeup: Keith AI demonstrates a distributed setup for Hermes that splits the local user interface from the inference backend hosted on a remote VPS. The local UI handles access on each device while the model compute stays centralized on the VPS, so one shared instance serves multiple devices. The takeaway: a single backend can back a local interface, separating where the interface runs from where the model runs.
